blockingIssues.description: PeerRow suppresses SIGN/ECDH/PING/ONBOARD badges when peer.online is false while PoliciesState always renders chips from effective_policy, creating deterministic cross-surface parity mismatch for offline peers.
Default policy application dispatches with direction "request" instead of the feature-specified inbound policy direction, so "Deny by default" cannot enforce the described respond-side semantics.
DefaultPolicyDropdown clears prior dropdown-owned keys on selection changes in a way that can clobber later manual overrides, violating the invariant that explicit user overrides remain unaffected.
policy-denial-roundtrip spec does not enforce the narrowed VAL-POLICIES-010 15-second bound (uses 60-second timeout), so slow failures can pass without meeting contract timing.
policy-denial-roundtrip spec does not assert that device A receives no pending sign operation for the denied request, leaving a required contract observable unverified.
Requested nonce-prepopulation test hook for deterministic denial setup was not implemented; only existing seed hook is exposed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a single mission-level source-of-truth note for policy-direction semantics (request vs respond) and require features/contract/library wording alignment before implementation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m3-default-policy-dropdown review found conflicting direction semantics across mission artifacts; implementation followed one interpretation while validation expectations referenced another.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require non-piped validator commands in worker verification logs (or explicit pipefail usage) so recorded exit codes cannot mask failing validators.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple m3 reviews observed handoff/transcript commands using `| tail`, which can report exitCode 0 while underlying tests are failing.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align ui-screen-worker/runtime-worker procedure text with AGENTS guidance for agent-browser requirements and `followedProcedure` reporting so mandatory checks are consistent and auditable.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m3 pass/fail reviews repeatedly flagged mismatch between mission AGENTS 'agent-browser mandatory' language and skill-level skip/omission patterns.

rejectedObservations.observation: Repeated variants of piped-command exit-code masking observation across multiple reports.
Repeated variants of agent-browser policy mismatch between AGENTS and worker skills.
RED-first sequencing mismatch report tied to feature-specific interface-expansion flow.
